papers.title: Uneven Evolution of Cognition Across Generations of Generative AI Models
papers.reason: Excluded because it evaluates the cognitive abilities of generative AI models rather than human participants.

papers.title: ASR-Agnostic Multimodal Spectrotemporal Modeling for Early Dementia Detection
papers.reason: It develops a human speech biomarker for dementia detection but lacks an interactive decision task, eye tracking, qualifying EEG analysis, or a cognitive process model.

papers.title: Demystifying Video Reasoning
papers.reason: Excluded because it studies reasoning and working memory inside diffusion-based video generation models rather than human cognition.

papers.title: The Richest Paradigm You're Not Using: Commercial Videogames at the Intersection of Human-Computer Interaction and Cognitive Science
papers.reason: It centrally proposes interactive commercial games with behavioral timing and eye tracking for studying human executive function, but does not report an empirical implementation.

papers.title: Pupillometry and Brain Dynamics for Cognitive Load in Working Memory
papers.reason: It analyzes human digit-span task behavior and pupillometry as cognitive-load biomarkers, while the reported EEG method lacks qualifying frequency-domain or connectivity analysis.

papers.title: Quant VideoGen: Auto-Regressive Long Video Generation via 2-Bit KV-Cache Quantization
papers.reason: Excluded because it concerns video generation and KV-cache memory in machine learning models rather than human cognition.

papers.title: Cognitive Load Estimation Using Brain Foundation Models and Interpretability for BCIs
papers.reason: It addresses continuous human EEG-based cognitive-load monitoring, but provides neither an interactive task description nor qualifying frequency-domain or connectivity analysis.

papers.title: Cross-Modal Computational Model of Brain-Heart Interactions via HRV and EEG Feature
papers.reason: It uses human working-memory tasks, but its EEG features are limited to spectral band power and its XGBoost and synthetic-HRV methods are not cognitive generative models.

papers.title: Unveiling the Heart-Brain Connection: An Analysis of ECG in Cognitive Performance
papers.reason: It includes a human working-memory task, but emphasizes ECG-to-EEG regression and band-power features rather than connectivity or detailed frequency-domain EEG analysis.

papers.title: Your Eyes Controlled the Game: Real-Time Cognitive Training Adaptation based on Eye-Tracking and Physiological Data in Virtual Reality
papers.reason: It combines interactive adaptive VR tasks targeting sustained attention and working memory with real-time eye tracking in human participants.

papers.title: How Light Shapes Memory: Beta Synchrony in the Temporal-Parietal Cortex Predicts Cognitive Ergonomics for BCI Applications
papers.reason: It analyzes human recall and sequence working-memory tasks using beta-phase EEG connectivity and regional synchrony.

papers.title: Setting up for failure: automatic discovery of the neural mechanisms of cognitive errors
papers.reason: Excluded because the neural validation uses macaque data, so the study population is not restricted to human participants.

papers.title: A Quantitative Framework for Assessing Sleep Quality from EEG Time Series in Complex Dynamic Systems
papers.reason: It combines a human working-memory task with EEG phase-amplitude coupling, spectral power, and connectivity analyses for individual assessment.

papers.title: Quantifying the Effects of Word Length, Frequency, and Predictability on Dyslexia
papers.reason: It centrally analyzes eye tracking in typical and dyslexic human readers, but naturalistic reading is not an interactive sequential decision task under the rubric.

papers.title: Working Memory Functional Connectivity Analysis for Dementia Classification using EEG
papers.reason: It combines staged human working-memory tasks with phase-lag and entropy-based EEG functional connectivity for AD, MCI, and control classification.

papers.title: Bayesian Distributional Models of Executive Functioning
papers.reason: Excluded because the evaluation uses controlled simulations and synthetically sampled test sessions rather than human participants.

papers.title: Bibliometric-enhanced Systematic Literature Review of EEG in Education: Learning Concepts, Computational Methods, and Research Opportunities
papers.reason: It reviews EEG-based assessment of human attention and learning but does not itself implement any of the four methodological elements.

papers.title: EEG-Based Framework for Reflexive and Perceptual Assessment in CLIS: Preliminary Study in Healthy Volunteers
papers.reason: It uses frequency-tagged SSAEP and SSVEP EEG responses in human volunteers, but the paradigms are passive and lack behavioral interaction.

papers.title: EEG-Based Cognitive Load Classification During Landmark-Based VR Navigation
papers.reason: It studies human decisions during interactive VR navigation, but reports EEG classification without qualifying frequency-domain or connectivity methodology.

papers.title: A Unified Cortical Circuit Model with Divisive Normalization and Self-Excitation for Robust Representation and Memory Maintenance
papers.reason: Excluded because it is a theoretical neural-circuit simulation without human participants or validation on human data.

papers.title: Dissociating Cognitive Load and Stress Responses Using Single-Channel EEG: Behavioral and Neural Correlates of Anxiety Across Cognitive States
papers.reason: It assesses cognitive load and stress in human adults, but the summary describes auditory protocols and EEG band summaries without a qualifying interactive task or detailed frequency-domain method.

papers.title: Sleep Brain and Cardiac Activity Predict Cognitive Flexibility and Conceptual Reasoning Using Deep Learning
papers.reason: It predicts human executive-function scores from passive sleep EEG and ECG, using power bands and deep learning rather than an analyzed task or cognitive process model.

papers.title: Evaluating Cognitive Assessment Tools:A Comparative Analysis of MMSE, RUDAS, SAGE, ADAS and MoCA for Early Dementia Detection
papers.reason: It reviews clinical cognitive assessment instruments for dementia but contains none of the specified behavioral, eye-tracking, EEG, or computational modelling methods.

papers.title: Early Detection of Cognitive Impairment in Elderly using a Passive FPVS-EEG BCI and Machine Learning -- Extended Version
papers.reason: It targets cognitive-impairment detection with passive EEG, but explicitly lacks behavioral interaction and reports CNN inference without detailed frequency-domain or connectivity analysis.

papers.title: Immersive Virtual Reality Assessments of Working Memory and Psychomotor Skills: A Comparison between Immersive and Non-Immersive Assessments
papers.reason: It validates interactive VR and PC versions of working-memory and reaction-time tasks in human participants, but includes no eye tracking, EEG, or cognitive process model.

papers.title: Triangulating LLM Progress through Benchmarks, Games, and Cognitive Tests
papers.reason: Excluded because it evaluates cognitive and game performance in large language models rather than human participants.

papers.title: Interactive Virtual Games: Winners for Deep Cognitive Assessment
papers.reason: It uses interactive cognitive minigames with continuous behavioral, movement, and gaze trajectories to characterize executive function in a human clinical sample.

papers.title: Mazed and Confused: A Dataset of Cybersickness, Working Memory, Mental Load, Physical Load, and Attention During a Real Walking Task in VR
papers.reason: It records eye tracking and continuous behavior while humans navigate VR mazes and perform attention and working-memory tasks, although cybersickness is the principal classification target.

papers.title: Pathformer3D: A 3D Scanpath Transformer for 360° Images
papers.reason: It centrally models human eye-tracking scanpaths from panoramic-image datasets, but uses passive viewing and a predictive transformer rather than a cognitive generative decision model.

papers.title: Transcranial low-level laser stimulation in near infrared-II region for brain safety and protection
papers.reason: It includes human EEG and executive-function assessments only as safety outcomes, without an interactive paradigm or qualifying EEG analysis.
